title: Multiline CUDA_CHECK lost the green closing delimiter
summary: CUDA_CHECK colored its outer parentheses with a same-line byte scan, so a closing parenthesis on a later row could never be found or restored.
details: The macro token still became the CUDA-green question-mark alias and the opening parenthesis was colored, making the uncolored close easy to miss as a separate failure.
Nested calls made a broader textual scan undesirable even if it crossed lines because it would need to reimplement balanced C++ syntax.
The correction asks Tree-sitter for the CUDA_CHECK call_expression's argument_list and paints only that node's exact opening and closing boundaries.
Each row independently discovers whether its parenthesis is a CUDA_CHECK boundary, so row-scoped reveal cleanup cannot leave the close unpainted until the opener repaints.
The delimiter priority remains below the active-scope pair but above ordinary ancestor-pair highlighting.

provenance.note: Owner reported that the closing parenthesis was not found and therefore was not green.
The prior loop iterated only from the opener through the current line and had no cross-row state.

evidence.claim: Both exact argument-list boundaries are CUDA-highlighted; selecting and leaving only the close row removes and independently restores its highlight.

evidence.reference: style_lab/catalog.json cuda-control-flow scene and real ext-linegrid capture
evidence.claim: Actual Neovim RGB output shows both outer delimiters in CUDA seafoam across separate rows.

questions: Why was the closing parenthesis of a multiline CUDA_CHECK not green?
How are CUDA_CHECK delimiters restored after Visual mode?

last_verified.method: Reported example reproduced, implementation replaced with syntax boundaries, interaction repaint tested, and real-grid colors inspected.
